source.title: Improved betulinic acid biosynthesis using synthetic yeast chromosome recombination and semi-automated rapid LC-MS screening

initial_request.research_problem: Screening large strain libraries generated by SCRaMbLE for improved biosynthesis of metabolites like betulinic acid is bottlenecked by the low throughput of standard LC-MS, which takes ~5 min per sample, making it impractical to find rare (~1%) improved strains.
initial_request.target_object: Betulinic acid (BA) biosynthesis pathway in yeast
initial_request.known_context: SCRaMbLE generates improved strains at a rate of approximately one in a hundred, but screening is limited for metabolites without selectable phenotypes. Standard LC-MS takes ~5 min per sample, limiting its use for screening large strain libraries.
constraints: SCRaMbLE generates improved strains at ~1% rate
Standard LC-MS takes ~5 min per sample
quantifiable_goals: Increase screening throughput to identify rare improved strains
Achieve at least 2-fold improvement in BA titre

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SCRaMbLE generates improved strains at ~1% rate, but screening is limited for metabolites without selectable phenotypes. Standard LC-MS takes ~5 min per sample, limiting screening of large strain libraries.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to rapidly screen large SCRaMbLE libraries for improved BA production?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Combining automated sample preparation, ultra-fast LC-MS, and barcoded nanopore sequencing will enable rapid isolation and characterization of improved strains.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a semi-automated workflow integrating these components.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Workflow combines automated sample preparation, an ultra-fast 84-second LC-MS method, and barcoded nanopore sequencing.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Implement the ultra-fast LC-MS method for BA detection.

evidence.quote_or_span: SCRaMbLE generates improved strains at a rate of approximately one in a hundred
the time required for each sample is significant, limiting its use for screening large strain libraries
we combine automated sample preparation, an ultra-fast 84-second LC-MS method, and barcoded nanopore sequencing to rapidly isolate and characterise the best performing strains

With a runtime of 72 s with 12 s of injection time, the guard column reduces the time for screening from $\sim$ 5 min per well ( $\sim$ 8 h per 96-plate) to 84 s per well (<2.3 h per 96-plate) offering a throughput advantage of $\sim$ 4-fold (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Workflow design proposed but ultra-fast LC-MS method not yet implemented.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a rapid LC-MS method for BA detection.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Using a guard column and a 72 s gradient will enable rapid BA detection.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop ultra-fast LC-MS method using a Zorbax Eclipse Plus C18 UHPLC guard column.

parameters.column: Zorbax Eclipse Plus C18 UHPLC guard column (2.1 × 5 mm, 1.8 μm)

evolution_trajectory.observation: Method designed with 72 s gradient, enabling 84 s per sample throughput.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Apply method to screen SCRaMbLE-induced colonies.

evidence.quote_or_span: A Zorbax Eclipse Plus C18 UHPLC guard column (2.1 × 5 mm, 1.8 μm particle size; Agilent Technologies, Santa Clara, CA, USA) was used for analysis.
The LC gradient was 72 s in total without post-time run as summarised in Supplementary Data 1.

With a runtime of 72 s with 12 s of injection time, the guard column reduces the time for screening from $\sim$ 5 min per well ( $\sim$ 8 h per 96-plate) to 84 s per well (<2.3 h per 96-plate) offering a throughput advantage of $\sim$ 4-fold (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Ultra-fast LC-MS method ready; SCRaMbLE strain library not yet screened.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to induce SCRaMbLE and screen colonies for improved BA production.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: SCRaMbLE induction with beta-estradiol will generate diverse strains, some with improved BA titre.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Induce SCRaMbLE for 4 h with 1 μM beta-estradiol, then screen 964 colonies.

tool_or_method.name: SCRaMbLE induction and ultra-fast LC-MS screening

evolution_trajectory.observation: 964 induced colonies were picked and screened.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze screening data to identify hits.

evidence.quote_or_span: 964 induced colonies were picked

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which strains show significantly improved BA titre?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Strains with BA titre >4σ above control mean are likely improved.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Set threshold at 4 standard deviations above control mean.

evidence.quote_or_span: A threshold for significant titre improvement was set 4 standard deviations above the mean control values
A total of 12 screened strains exceeded this threshold
964 induced colonies were picked

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Re-analysis will confirm improved BA titres.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Re-analyze the 12 strains using the same LC-MS workflow.
evolution_trajectory.observation: All 12 strains showed 2- to 7-fold higher BA titres than control.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Sequence the improved strains to identify genetic changes.

evidence.quote_or_span: All 12 strains did indeed show significantly higher BA titres of between two- and sevenfold above that of the yGG066 control
The top 12 BA-producing strains (BC01-12) identified by the screen were selected from the glycerol stock plates.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: 12 improved strains confirmed but genetic basis unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What SCRaMbLE events caused the improvement?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Nanopore sequencing will reveal structural variations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform Oxford Nanopore sequencing on BC01-12.

tool_or_method.name: Oxford Nanopore MinION sequencing
tool_or_method.version: SQK-LSK109 kit, EXP-NDB104 barcoding kit, R9.4.1 flowcell

evolution_trajectory.observation: Sequencing data obtained for all 12 strains.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze sequences to detect SCRaMbLE events.

evidence.quote_or_span: Extracted DNA from BC01-12 was prepared for Oxford Nanopore sequencing using genomic DNA kit SQK-LSK109 with 1D Native Barcoding kit EXP-NDB104 (Oxford Nanopore Technologies).
Sequencing was run for 48 h with local basecalling in MinKNOW.
All 12 strains did indeed show significantly higher BA titres of between two- and sevenfold above that of the yGG066 control
Our semi-automated workflow screens 1,000 colonies, identifying and sequencing 12 strains with between 2- to 7-fold improvement in betulinic acid titre.
The top 12 BA-producing strains (BC01-12) identified by the screen were selected from the glycerol stock plates.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify SCRaMbLE events from sequencing data.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Aligning reads to pre-SCRaMbLE synV and searching for LoxPsym boundaries will detect rearrangements.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use Porechop, LASTAL, and Tablet for analysis.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Pipeline detected rearrangements in some strains; BC03 had four events.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Characterize specific rearrangements in top strain BC03.

evidence.quote_or_span: Reads were demultiplexed using Porechop (https://github.com/rrwick/Porechop) and aligned to pre-SCRaMbLE synV sequence using LASTAL (-1100 flag) (http://last.cbrc.jp/).
Extracted DNA from BC01-12 was prepared for Oxford Nanopore sequencing using genomic DNA kit SQK-LSK109 with 1D Native Barcoding kit EXP-NDB104 (Oxford Nanopore Technologies).
The top 12 BA-producing strains (BC01-12) identified by the screen were selected from the glycerol stock plates.
3 h per 96-plate) offering a throughput advantage of $\sim$ 4-fold (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BC03 identified as top producer; rearrangement details unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What specific rearrangements occurred in BC03?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BC03 contains multiple SCRaMbLE events on chromosome V.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze BC03 sequencing data for structural variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: BC03 has four SCRaMbLE rearrangements: two deletions (551 bp, 391 bp), one inversion (10192 bp), one duplication (8724 bp).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Investigate functional impact of rearrangements, e.g., TIR1 deletion.

evidence.quote_or_span: Sequencing of this strain revealed a complex combination of four SCRaMbLE rearrangements on synthetic chromosome V.
Two non-coding deletions of 551 and 391 bp, respectively, were seen alongside a 10192 bp inversion and 8724 bp duplication
3 h per 96-plate) offering a throughput advantage of $\sim$ 4-fold (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: TIR1 3' UTR deletion identified in BC11; causal role unconfirmed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does TIR1 3' UTR deletion cause improved BA titre?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: CRISPR knockout of TIR1 3' UTR in parental strain will recapitulate improved BA titre.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use CRISPR to delete TIR1 3' UTR in yGG066.

tool_or_method.name: CRISPR knockout
tool_or_method.version: cas9 plasmid pWS2082, gRNA plasmid gGG002

parameters.donor_DNA: PCR amplification of dTIR1-3' region from BC11

evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate BA titre in engineered strain.

evidence.quote_or_span: Donor DNA was generated by PCR amplification of the dTIR1-3' region of BC11 using primers GG126 (GTATTCCTCACGATTAGAACCAGCC) and GG127 (CGTAAAGATTT CGCTGCGAAAG).

a The top 12 strains from the initial screen (BC01-BC12), and the control strain (yGG066), were subject to reanalysis with n = 7 (n = 6 for the control strain) biologically independent samples using the same extraction and LC-MS workflow as in the initial screen.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: CRISPR-engineered yGG066 with TIR1 3' UTR deletion created.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does the deletion confer the same BA titre improvement as BC11?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The engineered strain will show a threefold increase in BA titre, matching BC11.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure BA titre in engineered strain and compare to BC11.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Engineered strain showed same BA titre as BC11, confirming TIR1 3' UTR deletion causes threefold increase.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Hypothesize mechanism of TIR1 deletion effect.

evidence.quote_or_span: we used CRISPR-based genome engineering to artificially re-create this deletion in the control yGG066 strain, removing the TIR1 3'UTR region
We observe the same BA titre as in BC11, confirming that this single SCRaMbLE event is responsible for the threefold increase in BA biosynthesis.

a The top 12 strains from the initial screen (BC01-BC12), and the control strain (yGG066), were subject to reanalysis with n = 7 (n = 6 for the control strain) biologically independent samples using the same extraction and LC-MS workflow as in the initial screen.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: TIR1 deletion confirmed to improve BA titre; mechanism unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does TIR1 deletion increase BA accumulation?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BA accumulates at the cell wall, and changes to cell wall structure may affect BA accumulation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Further experiments needed to test hypothesis.

evidence.quote_or_span: We suspect that BA accumulates at the cell wall and therefore any changes to the cell wall structure may affect the accumulation of BA.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Sequencing of 12 strains completed; some lack detectable rearrangements.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Why did three strains show improved BA titre without detectable SCRaMbLE events?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Natural mutations elsewhere in the genome may have caused improvements.
evolution_trajectory.observation: No obvious rearrangement detected for three strains.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Future work could use whole-genome sequencing to identify mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: no obvious rearrangement was detected by our sequencing approach for three of the strains.
All 12 strains did indeed show significantly higher BA titres of between two- and sevenfold above that of the yGG066 control

validation_methods: Re-analysis of top 12 strains by LC-MS
CRISPR recapitulation of TIR1 3' UTR deletion
metrics: BA titre fold change relative to control
success_verification.final_conclusion: The semi-automated workflow successfully identified 12 strains with 2- to 7-fold improved BA titre. The top strain BC03 had four SCRaMbLE rearrangements. CRISPR validation confirmed that a TIR1 3' UTR deletion in BC11 caused a threefold increase in BA biosynthesis.
limitations: No obvious rearrangement detected for three of the top 12 strains, suggesting natural mutations elsewhere may have caused improvements.
